# BRIER-Agent

> An agent that drives BRIER (transfer-learning polygenic risk scores) through natural
> language, built to run with a small local model (Qwen 2.5-7B) so genetic data never
> leaves the machine. It inspects the data, infers the right BRIER module, prepares
> fit-ready inputs, runs the fit through R, compares transfer against no-transfer and
> external-only baselines, and reports held-out performance.

The model is reached over an OpenAI-compatible endpoint: a self-hosted vLLM (local, GPU,
private) or an external API (OpenAI, Together, ...). The agent code is identical for both.

## Start here

- [README](README.md): what it is, the two model backends, repository layout.
- [DEPLOY](DEPLOY.md): Docker deployment, both backends, the environment check.

## How it works

- [mcp/docs/AGENTS.md](mcp/docs/AGENTS.md): the BRIER workflow guide the model reads
  (module routing, the prepared-object contract, selection and evaluation policy).
- [mcp/README.md](mcp/README.md): the bundled BRIER-MCP server (tools, install, standalone
  use with Claude or Codex).

## Run

- Environment check: `python -m brier_agent.check_env`
- CLI: `python -m brier_agent "your request"`
- UI: `python app.py` (Gradio, port 7860)
